name: Mrs. Comfort Enders, M.Ed
bio: With a combination of high school biology teaching qualifications and experience, fourteen years of full-time Christian missionary work among university students in West Africa, nearly twenty years of K-12 leadership and teacher development, and university teaching/faculty development, Mrs. Enders provides strategic guidance for modeling and networking in Christian school culture, leadership, teacher development, and institutional growth.

name: Jonathan Enders, B.Sc
bio: Grounded in a fourteen-year experience in full-time christian missionary work among university students in West Africa, including Liberia, and a full college degree, Enders' role keeps the wheels constantly turning. He brings his project and people management skills to the array of services provided by KFI.

name: Benjamin Geeton, M.A.
bio:
Serves from a rich background of church and school leadership and teaching, having earned qualifications in education and, of recent, completing doctoral studies in Christian Education Leadership.
Geeton not only oversees the K-12 daily school operations as the building principal but is also one of the key facilitators in the training of teachers and administrators.

name: Isaac Dwana, B.BA
bio: With an accounting major from the University of Liberia and over a decade experience in financial management in local NGOs, community development projects, church, and schools, Dwana holds key management roles in financial, human resource, and infrastructure management at Kingdom Foundation Institute.

name: Abraham Kwimie, B.A.
bio: From an extensive experience in classroom teaching and two levels of qualifications in education, Kwimmie keeps teachers supported to meet their professional goals, maintain quality, and apply instructional best practices on a planned regular basis. He is one of the facilitators of the teacher continuing professional development activities.
